items.title: Trump stuns Saudis by pinning nuclear deal to Israel ties
items.description: US President Donald Trump said Thursday that a nuclear deal with Saudi Arabia depends on the kingdom recognizing Israel -- throwing the agreement into turmoil just the day after it was signed. Riyadh has long resisted joining the so-called Abraham Accords with Israel, insisting especially after the Gaza conflict on a pathway to a Palestinian state. FRANCE 24's Emerald Maxwell reports.

items.title: Hundreds hospitalised in Japan heatwave 'disaster'

items.title: Lenacapavir will slow HIV infections if there's enough of it and it reaches at-risk groups
items.description: Lenacapavir is one injection, twice a year. It's a big improvement for people in high-risk groups, currently taking a pill a day against HIV infection. But its long-term impact depends on its reaching those very people.

items.title: Spain battles spreading wildfires fuelled by climate-change driven heat

It's one of many wildfires ravaging across western Europe as parts of southern France and Italy are also ablaze. In 2025, more than 393,000 hectares had been consumed by flames in Spain, according to the European Forest Fire Information System. It is the worst toll in the country's recent history, fuelled by extreme temperatures cause by climate change.

items.description: Congo's Ebola outbreak has crossed the grim 1,000-death threshold, marking the fastest fatal surge in recorded history of the virus.

items.title: Ghanaians' ICC petition over migrant attacks 'opportunistic', says South Africa
items.description: The petition argues the violence against migrants in South Africa could amount to crimes against humanity.

items.title: Ex-governor convicted of murdering a pregnant student in Kenya
items.description: Okoth Obado was found guilty of killing Sharon Otieno with whom he had had an affair.

items.title: US military continues strikes on Iran as attacks overshadow diplomacy
items.description: The U.S. military said it completed the 11th night of strikes on Iran early Wednesday as attacks across the Middle East overshadowed diplomatic efforts in Pakistan to salvage an interim ceasefire deal that has collapsed.

items.title: Stocks sink on Big Tech cash burn; oil hits $100 for first time since May
What's next for the Albiceleste after World Cup final defeat?
items.description: Argentina fell agonisingly short of winning a second consecutive World Cup title. As the team reaches the end of an era, speculation begins over what comes next. Leer más

items.title: Hiding behind the World Cup, Milei siblings restructure their government
items.description: The World Cup provided the Milei administration a much-needed breather after three months under siege. Javier and Karina Milei are learning by doing, but by doing so they are slowly but surely resembling what they initially promised to battle. Leer más

items.title: Sweden eases mining permits to cut China minerals reliance
items.description: Sweden has declared the mining of critical minerals and rare earths a national security interest. This will ease and expedite the permit process. The government said the step was needed to counter dependence on China.

items.title: How Russia is luring foreign nationals to fight in Ukraine
items.description: More and more, Russia is recruiting men from the world’s poorest countries to send them to the front in the Ukraine war. Why are people joining up?

items.title: Messi denied dream farewell as Argentina disappoint in World Cup final
items.description: Skipper's dream of lifting the trophy again, four years after he inspired Argentina to victory in Qatar, ultimately proved too good to be true. Leer más

items.title: SA police say man dies while resisting arrest in Adelaide CBD
items.description: A man has died in Adelaide's CBD after police say he became unresponsive during an attempted arrest this afternoon.

items.title: Canada hopes to sign Mercosur free-trade deal this year
items.description: Canada and the Mercosur bloc, made up of Argentina, Bolivia, Brazil, Paraguay and Uruguay, resumed negotiations last year. Leer más

items.title: Threading a future: The woman weaving a new art form in Burkina Faso
items.description: In a country where painting and sculpture have long reigned supreme, one artist is threading a bold new vision—one nail, one strand, one masterpiece at a time

items.title: Turkey-PKK talks: Is peace finally possible?
items.description: A year ago, the Kurdish militant group PKK announced an end to its armed struggle. Since then, peace talks with Turkey have progressed slowly, but some observers believe they could succeed.

items.title: India, U.S. note ‘substantial progress’ on trade deal talks as U.S. Trade Representative Greer ends two-day visit to Delhi
items.description: U.S. Trade Representative Jamieson Greer held multiple meetings with Commerce Minister Piyush Goyal; however, the two sides have not yet been able to announce a deadline by which the trade deals will be finalised

items.title: Spain shatters Argentina's dream with 1-0 win in World Cup final
items.description: Disappointment for Argentina and skipper Lionel Messi in World Cup final; Barcelona striker Ferran Torres lashes home a thumping finish in the 106th minute to reward a dominant display and win Spain the World Cup. Leer más

items.title: Houthi attacks raise fears of wider Middle East conflict and more global economic damage
items.description: The Iran-backed group in Yemen has threatened to target Saudi vessels in a vital shipping route.

items.title: World Cup halftime performers see jump in song streams
items.description: It was the 11-minute performance heard around the world. Madonna, Justin Bieber, BTS and Shakira each brought abridged hits to the World Cup’s first halftime show in a whirlwind performance Sunday just outside New York. And it has resulted in some noteworthy streaming bumps.

items.title: US citizens are ramping up pressure on data centers
items.description: Rapid data center construction across the US, fueled by AI, comes with serious threats to the environment, public health and energy supply. Local opposition has blocked or delayed dozens of projects.

items.title: AliExpress hit with record EU fine
items.description: The Chinese retailer has become the latest major online marketplace to face penalties under the EU’s Digital Services Act.

items.title: Tearful fights, edible offerings – a glimpse into the Diego Maradona death trial
items.description: A look at the standout moments of an often dramatic case; Defendants, who insist Maradona died of natural causes, have spent months in a courtroom alongside the footballer's distraught daughters. Leer más

items.title: Argentina's World Cup heroics move me to tears, says goalkeeper Emiliano Martínez
items.description: Argentina keeper says he is enjoying this World Cup campaign much more than in 2022, despite coming into the tournament injured. Leer más

items.title: Oil prices jump back over $US100 a barrel as Middle East conflict widens
items.description: The entry of the Houthi rebels into the war between the US and Iran threatens another vital shipping route through the region, as the entire region remains on high alert.

items.title: Japanese sake brewery to make foray into Indian market
items.description: Takahashi Shouten, based in the city of Yame in Fukuoka Prefecture, launched a subsidiary in India in February.

items.title: How the student 'Cockroach' movement grew its influence across India
items.description: Young Indians are keeping up the pressure - continuing their demands for the Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan - to step down. This as their online satirical group the "Cockroach People's Party" grows in stature and support. It won millions of followers on social media and tens of thousands of sign-ups since its launch in May.

items.title: Milei almost halved education and science spending, reports finds
items.description: University of Buenos Aires researchers say education investment has almost halved in two years since President Javier Milei took office, while second Grupo EPC analysis says funding for science has fallen by more than 46%. Leer más

items.title: VIDEO: Enugu Air confirms runway excursion in Benin, no injuries reported
items.description: The airline said all 63 passengers and five crew members disembarked safely after the aircraft veered off the runway while landing at Benin Airport. The post VIDEO: Enugu Air confirms runway excursion in Benin, no injuries reported appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria .

items.title: From hotel bedsheet to World Cup pitch: the story of Argentina’s Malvinas flag
items.description: Daubed with ink on a hotel bedsheet, the Malvinas banner became one of the symbols of Argentina's dramatic World Cup win over England. Its creator, who spoke exclusively to Perfil and asked to remain anonymous, explained how the flag came about, why the squad won't give it back and why he'll be at Sunday's final. Leer más

items.title: Russia's businesses under strain from Ukraine's attacks on Wildberries
items.description: Ukrainian drones have hit several retail warehouses belonging to Russia's biggest online retailer Wildberries in a matter of days.

items.title: Lebanon PM says Beirut working for 'complete Israeli withdrawal'
items.description: The Israeli military said it had only fired "warning shots into the air" after Lebanese soldiers entered an area that was "not part of the pilot area".

items.title: Senegal set to make Olympic history with Dakar 2026 Youth Games
items.description: The Youth Olympic Games open in Dakar on 31 October. Africa has produced Olympic legends for decades, but has never hosted the Games themselves, until now.

items.title: US strikes on southern Iran infrastructure widen war
items.description: The latest US strikes on tunnels, bridges and other infrastructure further inland from the Strait of Hormuz could indicate that Washington is shifting gears in Iran.

items.title: Trump’s 200% generic tariff threat would imperil Americans: Indian pharma
items.description: Effective August 1, 2026, all generic drugs being brought into the United States will continue to have a tariff of zero per cent for a two-year period of time, after which the tariff will be raised to 100% for a one-year period of time, and 200% thereafter, said Donald Trump

items.title: Ethiopia opposition accuses government of pushing Tigray towards new war
items.description: Ethiopia's opposition People's Revolutionary Party has accused the federal government of pushing the northern Tigray region towards a new war. The EPRP says Addis Ababa is waging a propaganda campaign to psychologically prepare public opinion for conflict.

items.title: Adolescence creator swaps therapy for chains in new movie
items.description: Emmy Award-winning Adolescence co-creator Stephen Graham tackles teen delinquency in a very different way in The Good Boy.

items.title: Indian-American Amish Shah wins Democratic congressional primary from Arizona
items.description: Amish Shah, a three-term member of the Arizona House of Representatives, defeated Marlene Galan-Woods, a candidate backed by the Democratic Congressional Campaign Committee

items.title: Barcelona signs forward Karim Adeyemi from Borussia Dortmund
items.description: Spanish champion Barcelona signed forward Karim Adeyemi from Borussia Dortmund on a five-year contract on Thursday, as it rebuilds the attack following the departure of veteran striker Robert Lewandowski
